text.title: Reranking — Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure: measure recall ceiling → pick provider by constraints → tune top-N/top-K → wire fallback → eval + ship.

step.name: measure-recall-ceiling
step.action: Run recall@20 (or @50) without reranker. Verify ≥80%; if <80%, fix first-stage retrieval before adding reranker (reranker improves precision, not recall).
step.decision-gate: If recall@20 < 80% — STOP; reranker won't help (r1-reranker-after-retrieval).

step.name: pick-provider
step.input: latency budget, privacy requirement, monthly cost cap
step.action: Use decision tree: managed + latency-tight → Cohere/Voyage; on-prem + privacy → BGE local; batch / async → FlashRank. Pin model version.
step.decision-gate: If provider cap < needed top_n — pick different provider or reduce top_n (r2-top-n-capacity).

step.name: tune-top-n-top-k
step.action: Sweep top_n ∈ {20, 50, 100} × top_k ∈ {3, 5, 10}. Pick the (top_n, top_k) that maximises precision@K under latency budget.
step.decision-gate: If best precision violates latency budget — reduce top_n; do not silently exceed budget (r3-latency-budget).

step.name: wire-fallback
step.input: health-check endpoint + local BGE container OR first-stage handler
step.action: Implement fallback: on 3 consecutive 5xx OR timeout > 2s, switch to fallback strategy. Test with synthetic outage in staging.
step.decision-gate: If fallback latency > budget × 2 — accept degraded mode banner; don't try to mask (r5-fallback-on-outage).

step.name: eval-and-ship
step.action: Final run on hold-out set: precision_lift ≥ 0.10 vs no-reranker baseline. Document in eval_evidence. Ship behind feature flag at 5% → 25% → 100%.
step.decision-gate: If precision_lift < 0.10 — STOP; reranker not justified (r4-eval-before-adopt).
